title: Computed Properties 🧮
description: Variables that instantly calculate their own value when you ask for it.

slides.title: Lazy vs Smart 🧮
slides.content:
# Stored vs Computed

The properties we've made so far (like `name` and `score`) are called **Stored Properties**. They just sit there lazily holding data.

But we can also create **Computed Properties**. Instead of storing a value, these properties calculate their value dynamically every single time they are accessed!

They look a lot like methods, but they behave like variables.

slides.title: Building a Calculator 🧮

quizData.question: What happens if an Employee's salary changes from 52,000 to 104,000?

options.text: weeklyPay remains completely unchanged because it was already set.
weeklyPay instantly recalculates a higher amount the next time you print it.

quizData.explanation: Because it doesn't store data, a Computed Property runs its math fresh every single time you invoke it. It's always perfectly up to date!

slides.title: See It in Action
slides.content: Change the `width` or `height` below, and watch the `area` automatically recalculate itself!
